doc_title: EDPB Guidance Note 04/2026 on Pseudonymized Health Data Processed by Non-Holders of the Linkage Key
label: fabricated
reason: No such EDPB guidance note exists in the EDPB's published record, the superseded document cited is itself fictitious, and the core legal claim that pseudonymised data held by a non-key-holder falls entirely outside GDPR's material scope dir
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 94
REASON: No such EDPB guidance note (04/2026, adopted 22 March 2026) exists in the EDPB's published record; the cited case C-604/22 (*IAB Europe*, 7 March 2024) is real, but the core legal claim — that pseudonymised data held by a non-key-holder falls entirely *outside* GDPR's material scope — directly contradicts Recital 26 GDPR and settled EDPB doctrine, and the reference to a superseded "EDPB Guidance 05/2014 on anonymisation techniques" is itself a fabrication (that document does not exist under that designation), all of which strongly indicate this is an AI-generated, invented document rather than authentic institutional material.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 94
REASON: No such EDPB guidance note exists in the EDPB's published record, the superseded document cited is itself fictitious, and the core legal claim that pseudonymised data held by a non-key-holder falls entirely outside GDPR's material scope directly contradicts Recital 26 GDPR and settled EDPB doctrine.
